texto: They say you’re meant to live everyday as if it were your last, which I’ve always thought was daft, since no one would ever pay the gas bill if that was the case, but what if it were your first?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amy Jenkins: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “They say you’re meant to live everyday as if it were your last, which I’ve always thought was daf…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, thoughts, meant pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de thoughts.

frase_original: They say you’re meant to live everyday as if it were your last, which I’ve always thought was daft, since no one would ever pay the gas bill if that was the case, but what if it were your first?

texto: Oh I believe in loving cats and dogs and children and parents – sometimes – but I don’t believe in romantic love. Of course, there’s the momentary rush of hormones and chemicals that encourages us to mate, but it’s biology – it’s no more inherently mystical than the nicotine in that cigarette you’re smoking
